Texas · State Licensing Reference

Speech-Language Pathologist
Licensed Speech-Language Pathologist (SLP) Guide

Issued by the Speech-Language Pathologists and Audiologists Program under the Texas Department of Licensing and Regulation (TDLR). Data verified for 2026.

Visit Official Board Portal
Fees Board Contact License Types How to Apply Documents Telepractice Reciprocity FAQ
Total Initial Fee
$117
$80 application + $37 fingerprint/background
Renewal
$80
Every 2 years + 20 CE hours
Processing Time
2 to 4 Weeks
Subject to board volume
License Duration
2 Years
From date of issuance
Board Contact

Speech-Language Pathologists and Audiologists Program

Austin, TX 78711

920 Colorado St.
Austin, TX 78701

Phone & Fax

Fax: (512) 463-9468

Email

cs.speech@tdlr.texas.gov

Credential Categories

Texas SLP License Types

Licensed Speech-Language Pathologist (SLP)

The standard credential to practice speech-language pathology in Texas. Requires a master's degree, Praxis exam, and completion of a supervised Clinical Fellowship (CF).

SLP Intern License

For Clinical Fellows completing their post-graduate supervised experience. Valid during the CF period under an ASHA-certified mentor.

Application Process

How to Apply for a Texas Licensed Speech-Language Pathologist (SLP)

Complete a Master's Degree in SLP

Earn a master's degree from a CAA-accredited speech-language pathology program.

Pass the Praxis Examination in SLP

Achieve a passing score on the Praxis Examination in Speech-Language Pathology (test code 5331). The current passing score is 162.

Complete the Clinical Fellowship

Complete a minimum of 36 weeks of full-time supervised clinical practice (1,260 hours) under an ASHA-certified SLP.

Apply Online via TDLR

Submit your application through the TDLR Online Licensing System. Pay the $80 application fee and complete the fingerprint-based background check ($37).

Required Documents

Application Document Checklist

  • Official graduate transcriptsFrom a CAA-accredited program.
  • Praxis exam score reportScore of 162 or higher on test 5331.
  • Clinical Fellowship verificationCF supervisor attestation or ASHA CCC-SLP certificate.
  • Fingerprint submissionCompleted via the TDLR FAST Pass system.
Telepractice

Texas Telepractice Rules for SLPs

Telehealth Practice

Texas permits telepractice for licensed SLPs. The practitioner must hold a valid Texas SLP license regardless of where they are physically located. Services delivered to patients in Texas require Texas licensure.

Practice Laws

Provisional Practice & Reciprocity

Clinical Fellowship & Provisional Rules

Texas issues an SLP Intern License for Clinical Fellows. This allows supervised practice during the CF period. The intern must be directly supervised by an ASHA-certified SLP holding a valid Texas license.

Reciprocity & Endorsement

Texas does not have formal reciprocity agreements. Out-of-state SLPs must apply for a new Texas license, submit proof of their master's degree, Praxis score, and completed Clinical Fellowship. If you hold current ASHA CCC-SLP certification, this streamlines the process as it demonstrates you have met national standards.

Frequently Asked Questions

Texas SLP FAQ

How much does a Texas SLP license cost?

The initial application fee is $80, paid to TDLR. You must also pay approximately $37 for the fingerprint-based background check, bringing the total to about $117.

Does Texas require the Praxis exam for SLPs?

Yes. All initial applicants must pass the Praxis Examination in Speech-Language Pathology (test code 5331) with a score of 162 or higher.

Can I practice SLP via telepractice in Texas?

Yes. Texas permits telepractice for licensed SLPs. You must hold a valid Texas SLP license to provide services to patients located in Texas, regardless of where you are physically located.
